title: Master of Social Work
content: Master of Social Work programme offered by the Galgotias University is a 02-year Post Graduate academic degree programme, where students study about social welfare, social justice and diversity, human behaviour, social policy, social ethics, and other social practicalities and challenges. The programme focusses on imparting education, training and employability in professional social work in order to create and providing professionally trained human resource and allied fields through ethical approach, culture-sensitive, eclectic and evidence-based participatory practice at various levels and help students develop knowledge, skills, attitudes and values appropriate to the practices of social work profession and stimulate and encourage integration of theory and practice in the fields of social work profession; and special focus to facilitate and provide interdisciplinary collaboration for better understanding of social issues, social problems, issues of social development and needed services Graduation with minimum 50% marks. Students with a CUET score will be given preference in the merit list. Students can pursue variouscareer options after completing their master of Social work programme. Some of popular career options are as given below. 01 To Develop a comprehensive understanding of advanced social work theories, human behavior, social policies, and welfare systems relevant to contemporary social issues. 02 To Build practical competencies in assessment, intervention, counseling, case management, and community mobilization across various fields of practice such as health, education, family, mental health, and development. 03 To Instill professional ethics, social work values, and a strong commitment to human rights, dignity, and social justice. 04 To Encourage critical thinking and application of qualitative and quantitative research methods for evidence-based social work interventions and policy formulation. 05 To enhance students to take up leadership roles in governmental, non-governmental, and international organizations, and advocate for social change and policy reform. 06 To Provide intensive fieldwork training to apply theoretical knowledge to real-world problems and develop professional identity and confidence. 07 To Equip students to work effectively with diverse populations, especially marginalized and vulnerable groups, respecting their social, cultural, and economic contexts. 08 To Enable students to pursue careers in professional social work practice, research, policy, and higher education. 01 Identify as a Professional Social Worker 02 Engage Diversity in Practice 03 Apply Critical Thinking 04 Apply Ethical Principles 05 Advance Human Rights/ Social and Economic Justice 06 Engage Research Informed Practice/Practice Informed Research 07 Apply Human Behaviour Knowledge 08 Engage Policy Practice to Advance Well-Being and Deliver Services 09 Evaluate Practice with Individuals, Families, Groups, Organizations, and Communities 10 Respond to Practice Contexts, Practice Engagement, Practice Assessment, Practice Assessment, Practice Evaluation 01 Social workers have an obligation to conduct themselves ethically and to engage in ethical decision making. Social workers are knowledgeable about the value and base of the profession, its ethical standards, and relevant law. 02 Social Workers serve as representatives of the profession, its mission, and its core values. They know the profession’s history. Social workers commit themselves to the profession’s enhancement and to their own professional conduct and growth 01 To impart education and training in professional social work in order to provide manpower in social welfare, development and allied fields capable of working at various levels of micro, meso and macro systems 02 To enable students develop creative thinking and ability to apply theoretical knowledge in practice of social work and to facilitate interdisciplinary approach for better understanding of social problems, situations and issues of development.
